rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 29, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG, GOLDBERG, JUNA, AS THE PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF, JUNA GOLDBERG'S Mtn To Tax Costs The motion is granted and denied in part. The Court strikes $10,998.19 in expert fees and $1,987.53 for records obtained by subpoena. Those expert costs were not incurred in "preparation for trial" within the meaning of CCP 998(c)(1). The Court denies as to the travel costs. They are recoverable under CCP 1033.5(a)(3) and defendant sufficiently establishes the costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BEAL et al VS. ROYAL OAK BAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (*Amended) Notice Of Moiton To Compel Plf'S To Submit A Dna Sample
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR MONDAY, APRIL 29, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT ARES PAPAGEORGE'S(*Amended) MOTION To Compel PlAINTIFfS To Submit A Dna Sample. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. While no opposition has been filed, the Court finds that there is no authority under California law for DNA testing under the facts of this case and that less intrusive means of obtaining the evidence at issue are available to Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BEAL et al VS. ROYAL OAK BAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Tpg Capital Corporation To Produce Employment Records
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR MONDAY, APRIL 29, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 1, DEFENDANT ARES PAPAGEORGE'S Motion To Compel Tpg Capital Corporation To Produce Employment Records. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. The Court notes that the subpoenaed party (TPG Capital) has requested that the documents be produced pursuant to a protective order, however, there is no request from any of the parties to this case for such an order. Since Plaintiff filed no opposition to Defendant's motion, nor a response to TPG's filing, the Court finds that Plaintiff has consented to TPG's production of the employment records at issue without limitation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN BENGTSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO SUPPLY MASTER, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independant Psychiatric Evaluation With Dr. Mark Strassberg,
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR MONDAY, APRIL 29, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 2, DEFENDANT CORD INVESTMENT COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Independant Psychiatric Evaluation With Dr. Mark Strassberg. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. The Court believes that any challenge to the validity of the written component of the psychiatric examination, and Plaintiff's ability to perform the tests while taking pain medication, should be made at trial rather than in a discovery motion. Sanctions denied. rulings.Case Title: BARBARA GRIFFIN VS. EURO AUTO IMPORTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 29, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT GREAT AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Ms. Griffin has not pled sufficient facts to allege that GAIC is directly liable for the acts of Euro Auto Imports, Inc. To the extent that GAIC may be liable per Vehicle Code § 11711, Ms. Griffin is granted leave to amend, if she can do so in good faith. The demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend; Ms. Griffin has stated that she will not pursue this cause of action against GAIC. For the third cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to plead sufficient facts to assert a claim on the bond pursuant to Vehicle Code §11711(a)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GRIGSBY & ASSOCIATES, INC. VS. ROBERT CERESA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Grigsby & Associates, Inc., Calvin Grigsby, Jerry Liang'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 29, 2013, Line 23, Plaintiffs Grigsby & Associates, Inc., Calvin Grigsby, Jerry Liang'S Motion For Summary Judgment The motion is denied in its entirety. Regarding the motion for summary adjudication, Plaintiff has failed to shift the burden with admissible evidence. Further, Plaintiffs' separate statement fails to comply with CCP § 437c(b)(1) or California Rule of Court 3.1350(d), which is grounds alone to deny the motion. Plaintiffs have listed some facts and stated that such facts can be found in the Complaint and Exhibits and the Grigsby Declaration. This is contrary to the rules governing motions for summary judgment and is inadequate. The Complaint and exhibits are 483 pages long, and were not even provided with the moving papers. Additionally, Plaintiffs failed to present any admissible evidence of the content of the Grigsby & Associates, Inc. bylaws or any purported forum selection clause contained therein. Regarding the alternative motion for preliminary injunction, Plaintiffs have failed to show a probability of prevailing or irreparable harm. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Deft Edward Litke
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR MONDAY, APRIL 29, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 3, PLAINTIFF REAL ESTATE EBROKER, INC.'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of DefENDANT Edward Litke. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted with the qualification that the deposition shall not occur before May 13, 2013. The Court finds that Defendant's doctor's note excused his attendance from a deposition in April, but does not excuse attendance indefinitely, especially given the upcoming trial date and lack of information regarding Defendant's current condition. If Defendant continues to contend that his medical condition prevents him from attending a deposition, Defendant's counsel shall provide to Plaintiff's counsel, and the Court, an updated medical evaluation from Defendant's physician no later than May 7, 2013. A further hearing on this matter will occur on May 24, 2013 at 9:00 am in Department 302 if a dispute over the deposition remains outstanding at that time. Sanctions denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VIOLET A. BOYD VS. PETER LIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 29, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT PETER LIP, L.AC. MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Judge Miller discloses (and will disclose at any hearing) that she is a member of the same Inn of Court as Plaintiff's counsel Ms. Van Vleet. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case, and Judge Miller believes that a person aware of the facts would not reasonably believe that she would be able to be impartial. The motion to strike is granted, with 10 days leave to amend. Ms. Boyd is granted leave to amend, if she can do so in good faith, to allege additional facts that demonstrate malicious conduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES J. CAMPBELL et al VS. AMERICANTOURS INTERNATIONAL, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 29, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT AMERICANTOURS INTERNATIONAL, LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue The motion to transfer venue is denied. Defendant has failed to carry its burden of establishing that the convenience of non-party, non-employee witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by a change of venue to Los Angeles County. CCP § 397(c). Richfield Hotel Management, Inc. v. Superior Court, 22 Cal. App. 4th 222, 226 (1994) is distinguishable. In that case, all of the potential witnesses lived in the transferee county. No such showing has been made here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
